reasoning: The Lean statement `Nat.card (AddAut (ZMod p)) = p - 1` with `[Fact p.Prime]` correctly formalizes $|\operatorname{Aut}(\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z})| = p - 1$. `AddAut (ZMod p)` is the group of additive automorphisms of the cyclic group $\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z}$, which is the standard meaning of $\operatorname{Aut}(\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z})$ when treating it as a group (the ring automorphism group would be trivial for the prime field). The primality hypothesis `[Fact p.Prime]` surfaces the implicit premise conveyed by the conventional use of letter $p$ — without primality, the claim is false (for general $n$, $|\operatorname{Aut}(\mathbb{Z}/n\mathbb{Z})| = \varphi(n)$). Making this implicit premise explicit is faithful, not weakening. The conclusion uses `Nat.card` (finite cardinality) and natural subtraction, which correctly represent $|\cdot|$ and $p-1$ for prime $p \geq 2$.
